solution.p: We will factor 121r^{2}+66ry+9y^{2} where a=121, b=66, and c=9.

Perfect Square Formula: The formula for perfect square trinomials is (a \pm b)^2=a^2 \pm 2ab + b^2. Note that the polynomial 121r^{2}+66ry+9y^{2} matches the formula's right side, and we have: \begin{aligned}[t]121r^{2}+66ry+9y^{2}\amp =\left(11r\right)^2+2\left(11r\right)\left(3y\right)+\left(3y\right)^2\newline \amp =\left(11r+3y\right)\mathopen{}\left(11r+3y\right)\newline \amp =\left(11r+3y\right)^{2}\end{aligned}
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (121) \cdot (9) = 1089.

The number 1089 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(1089)\amp -1(-1089)\amp 3(363)\amp -3(-363)
ewline9(121)\amp -9(-121)\amp 11(99)\amp -11(-99)
ewline33(33)\amp -33(-33)\amp \end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of 66, and this sum happens with 33+33=66 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]121r^{2}+66ry+9y^{2}\amp =121r^{2}+33ry+33ry+9y^{2}
ewline \amp =11r\mathopen{}\left(11r+3yight)+3y\mathopen{}\left(11r+3yight)
ewline \amp =\left(11r+3yight)\mathopen{}\left(11r+3yight)
ewline \amp =\left(11r+3yight)^{2}\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in 121r^{2}+66ry+9y^{2} into the top left and bottom right rectangles:
The first term's coefficient, 121, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(121)\amp 11(11)\amp \end{array}
The third term's coefficient, 9, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(9)\amp -1(-9)\amp 3(3)\amp -3(-3)\newline\end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with 121r^{2}+66ry+9y^{2} by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:

Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]121r^{2}+66ry+9y^{2}\amp =\left(11r+3y\right)\mathopen{}\left(11r+3y\right)\newline \amp =\left(11r+3y\right)^{2}\end{aligned}
